headings.styling: tracking-tighter uppercase (for small labels), bold presence, never centered unless specific hero layout. The 'Voice' of precision.

visual_enhancers: Textured noise overlay: Use CSS radial gradients or a subtle grain PNG overlay mixed with `mix-blend-overlay` to give a raw, metallic feel.
Gradient Meshes: Very dark, subtle background flares mixing #0B1F3A and #121212.
Grid Borders: Expose the skeleton for industrial technical look. Use border-collapse with subtle 1px border-[#2E2E2E]/40.
Glassmorphism for all floating elements (Nav, Cards, Modals). NEVER use 100% transparent backgrounds.

grids.marketing: MODE A: 'Tetris' Grid. md:grid-cols-12. gap-8. Heavy asymmetry. Span logic: Hero col-span-8, Secondary col-span-4.
grids.dashboard: MODE B: High Density. md:grid-cols-3. gap-4 or 6. Strict hierarchy.

layouts.spacing: Generous Spacing Philosophy. Use p-8 to p-16. Containers with borders MUST have internal padding. Optical alignment over mathematical.
layouts.sections: Rich and layered. Combine dark solid backgrounds with glassmorphism overlays.

motion.scroll: Smooth scroll (Lenis recommended if customized, otherwise rely on Framer Motion viewport triggers).
motion.interactions: Hover: Subtle scale-up (1.02) and brightness increase. Image hover: zoom effect (`group-hover:scale-110 transition-transform duration-700`).
motion.entrance: Staggered fade-up for list items using Framer Motion.

accessibility.contrast: Text over images must use heavy black linear gradients underneath. Active states must contrast against the dark theme.

media.images: Must have rounded corners (rounded-xl or 2xl), subtle inner shadow or border-white/10 to frame them, hover zoom effect via overflow-hidden wrappers.
media.overlays: Images must have dark overlays (bg-black/40) when text is placed on them.

image_urls.description: Worker generating bright sparks in dark industrial setting.
Woman engineer working on machine repair.

instructions_to_main_agent: DO NOT use pure white backgrounds (#FFFFFF) anywhere. Stick strictly to the dark theme (#121212).
Use Poppins for Headings with `font-black` and `tracking-tighter`.
Use Industrial Orange (#FF6B00) sparingly for CTAs, glowing borders, and key interactive elements.
Implement the 'Tracing Beam' or subtle glowing borders on Cards for the technical look.
For the Admin panel, use a high-density 'Bento' grid, strict spacing, and high contrast against the dark background.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed.
Components MUST use named exports (`export const ComponentName = ...`). Pages MUST use default exports (`export default function PageName() {...}`).
Use `sonner` for toasts. Sonner component are located in `/app/src/components/ui/sonner.js` (or similar).
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute.
